chapters.notes: Urgent opening, rescue from present harmful age, no rival/distorted good news, human approval trap, good news through unveiling/apocalypse of Jesus Messiah, former zeal, Arabia/Damascus/Jerusalem narrative, former pursuer now announces trust.

Jerusalem meeting, Titus not compelled to circumcision, false siblings spying freedom, right hand of fellowship, remembering the poor, Cephas confrontation at Antioch, table fellowship, justification/making-right through trust/faithfulness of Messiah, crucified with Messiah.

Spirit received by hearing of trust, beginning in Spirit not completed by flesh, Abraham trusted and was counted righteous/just, nations blessed in Abraham, Law/curse and Messiah release, promise before Law, paidagogos/guardian, baptism into Messiah, Judean/Greek enslaved/free male/female one in Messiah.

translation_notes: Galatians should preserve Paul's urgent freedom argument without flattening it into anti-Torah or anti-Jewish contempt.
Pistis should usually be trust/faithfulness; pisteuo trust; dikaiosyne righteousness/justice; dikaioo make righteous/just; charis grace; eleutheria freedom/liberty.
Nomos should be Law/Torah/law according to context. Paul's critique targets Law as boundary/regime for Gentile inclusion and flesh-pattern boasting, not Jewish identity as evil.
Sarx should be flesh/flesh-pattern according to context; avoid body-hatred. Pneuma should be Spirit/breath.
Stoicheia should be elemental powers/principles according to context.
Circumcision/uncircumcision should be treated as covenant-boundary markers in the argument, not as contempt for Jewish practice.
Agape should be love; Galatians 5:6 trust working through love is a key interpretive anchor.
Mystical companion layer remains interpretive and does not replace source-audited restored text.
Verse-number alignment must remain intact in both restored and mystical companion files.

Galatians 1-3 establishes the opening freedom argument: no rival good news, revelation of Messiah, table-fellowship conflict, trust/faithfulness rather than works of Law, Abraham promise, Spirit received through trust, Law as guardian, and baptismal oneness in Messiah.
next_actions: Continue Galatians 4-6: heirs and elemental powers, Abba Spirit, Hagar/Sarah allegory, freedom not slavery, trust working through love, Spirit versus flesh-pattern, fruit of Spirit, burden-bearing, sowing/reaping, new creation.

Add glossary entries or reuse hooks: pistis, pisteuo, dikaiosyne, dikaioo, charis, eleutheria, nomos, sarx, pneuma, stoicheia, circumcision/uncircumcision, agape, paidagogos, promise, seed, and new creation.
